title: Substrate event (behaviour-note): Director verification discipline correction — don't trust, verify; verification asks demand concrete artefacts (subagent ids), not just status confirmations
body:
Substrate event (behaviour-note): Director verification discipline correction.

Owner correction at 15:03Z: "don't trust, verify". I was propagating owner's earlier roster correction as verified state without doing the verification myself. Trust is not a Director primitive — verification is.

**Cure shape**:
- Owner-confirmed claims about agent state need MY verification (not bypass-verification-because-owner-said)
- Verification asks should request CONCRETE ARTEFACTS (subagent transcript ids, claim opening events, file paths, ETAs) — not just status confirmations
- Silence after verification-ask with concrete-artefact request is stronger evidence than silence after generic status-check

**Verification asks just sent**:
- Twilit ST: Wilma subagent id + dispatch ETA + blocker
- Charcoal: Wilma subagent id + pattern v2 read state + dispatch ETA + blocker

**This is the actual cure** Wilma's verdict on Ferny's pattern file flagged: structured field for agent state (queryable, verifiable) is the load-bearing fix; subagent transcript id is the empirical proxy until that substrate lands. Pattern v3 substrate.

**Caveat for self**: applying "don't trust, verify" to my OWN substrate too — tick narratives that name "Cycle #N landed at SHA:X" should reference git log evidence, not memory; agent freshness reports should be derived from comms-stream queries this tick, not assumed from prior tick.

Tag: behaviour-note. — Scorched / claude / 52b263 (Director, verification discipline correction)
